seoTitle: How to Connect a Custom Domain to Wix
metaDescription: Learn how to point a domain from GoDaddy, Namecheap, or another registrar to your Wix website by updating DNS records.
intro: If you bought your domain from a registrar like GoDaddy or Namecheap rather than Wix directly, you'll need to connect it manually by updating your DNS settings. This guide covers the pointing method, from entering your domain in Wix to updating nameserver records with your external registrar.

steps.heading: Open your domain settings
steps.body: In your Wix dashboard, navigate to the Domains subtab to start a new external connection.

steps.heading: Choose Connect a Domain You Already Own
steps.body: Select this option to begin linking an externally registered domain.

steps.heading: Enter your domain and registrar
steps.body: Input your domain name and let Wix identify your hosting registrar automatically.

steps.heading: Review the DNS modification steps
steps.body: Wix generates a step-by-step map of the DNS changes needed specifically for your registrar.

steps.heading: Update your nameserver records
steps.body: Log into your external domain registrar account and update the nameserver values as instructed.

steps.heading: Verify the connection
steps.body: Return to Wix and click Verify Connection, then monitor propagation until your domain is fully live with SSL.

faq.question: How long does DNS propagation take?
faq.answer: It can take anywhere from a few minutes to 48 hours depending on your registrar and network, so some waiting is normal.

faq.question: Do I need to transfer my domain to Wix?
faq.answer: No, you can either transfer full registration or simply point your existing domain's DNS to Wix, which is generally the faster option.

faq.question: Will my site have SSL after connecting the domain?
faq.answer: Yes, once the connection is verified and fully propagated, Wix issues an SSL certificate for your custom domain automatically.

faq.question: What happens to my site during the DNS propagation period?
faq.answer: There may be some downtime or inconsistent access while records propagate, so it's worth planning the switch during low-traffic hours.
